title: Bug Triage
description: Use when a bug report, crash, regression, failed test, or production symptom needs severity, reproducibility assessment, impact scope, suspected cause areas, and next diagnostic actions.

bug_classes.Crash/hang: Needs stack trace, dump, watchdog, and reproduction path.
bug_classes.Regression: Needs last known good version, changed commits, and failing workflow.
bug_classes.Data corruption/loss: Needs before/after records, audit logs, and backups; stop writes if ongoing.
bug_classes.Security/privacy: Needs access path, actor, exposed data, and audit logs; contain access quickly.
bug_classes.Performance: Needs latency, throughput, CPU/memory/I/O, and profile or plan evidence.
bug_classes.Flaky/intermittent: Needs repeated runs, timing, environment, and race/concurrency signals.
bug_classes.UI/UX defect: Needs browser/device, viewport, interaction path, and screenshot.
bug_classes.Integration failure: Needs dependency status, request/response, retry behavior, and error codes.

reproduction_strategy.Always: Reduce to shortest deterministic path and add regression test target.
reproduction_strategy.Intermittent: Collect repeated attempts, timing, random seed, concurrency, logs, and environment deltas.
reproduction_strategy.Environment-specific: Capture OS/browser/device/version/config/tenant/region and compare against known-good environment.
reproduction_strategy.Unknown: Define minimum reproduction checklist before assigning root cause or fix.

instructions: Classify bug class before severity and state why that class fits.
Extract observable facts: affected user/action, symptom, expected behavior, first known occurrence, frequency, environment, version/build, logs/errors, recent changes, and affected scope.
Separate evidence from inference and mark missing data explicitly.

Assign severity using impact and urgency: S0 for outage/data/security/no workaround, S1 for major blocked critical workflow, S2 for degraded important function with workaround or limited audience, S3 for minor or rare defects.
Assign confidence as High only with direct logs or repro steps, Medium with consistent symptoms, and Low with sparse or conflicting evidence.
Assess reproducibility as Always, Intermittent, Environment-specific, or Unknown, and apply the matching reproduction strategy.
List suspected cause areas in priority order with evidence for and against; do not present guesses as root cause.
Define next actions: data to collect, owner type, diagnostic test/log/query, mitigation for S0/S1, and escalation trigger.

examples.input: App crashes when opening empty workspace.
examples.output: Summary: opening an empty workspace crashes before project metadata loads. Severity: S1 if this blocks startup for all new workspaces; S2 if existing workspaces still work. Reproducibility: Unknown until tested with a freshly created empty workspace on the reported build. Evidence: crash symptom only; no stack trace yet. Suspected Areas: workspace metadata loader, empty project index handling, startup route guard. Next Actions: reproduce with a new empty workspace, capture stack trace, add regression test for empty metadata path, then guard the loader if confirmed.
